- The blue cursor (id=87) is still there.
- The yellow objects remain unchanged.
So clicking the middle gray control removed the middle vertical bar. This suggests the gray bars are "switches" that can be toggled or removed.
Let me check what the gray controls look like:
- Left: row 42, col 26 (horizontal) and row 45, col 26 (vertical) - still present
- Middle: row 42, col 36 (horizontal) and row 45, col 36 (vertical) - the vertical one is now gone
- Right: row 42, col 41 (horizontal) and row 45, col 41 (vertical) - still present
So clicking on the middle vertical bar removed it. The horizontal bar at row 42, col 36 is still there but the vertical one at row 45, col 36 is gone.
Wait, let me re-check. Before:
- id=85 color=w pixels=3 boundary=[[44, 36], [46, 36]] - middle vertical bar
After:
- id=87 color=w pixels=3 boundary=[[44, 41], [46, 41]] - this is the right vertical bar

So after clicking, the middle vertical bar disappeared. This means clicking on a gray control removes it.
